title: Mapping Housing and Food Insecurity in San Antonio
summary: A block-group-level map of Bexar County integrating five public datasets to find where housing cost burden and food access actually overlap.
detail: Integrated 5 public datasets — Census ACS, USDA Food Desert Atlas, USDA SNAP retailers, and food bank / WIC clinic locations — across ~1,139 Bexar County block groups.
Built a composite housing insecurity score from renter cost burden, severe cost burden, and overcrowding.
Used a KD-tree spatial index to compute food-access distances at 1-mile and 0.5-mile thresholds.
Produced a 4-quadrant overlap map classifying every block group by housing and food-access status.
Automated data refreshes with 3 GitHub Actions pipelines, keeping ongoing maintenance under 1 hour a year.
Shipped as a live interactive Leaflet.js map on GitHub Pages.

title: ITS Comparative Benchmarking
summary: Gap analysis of Trinity's IT services against 75 peer and aspirant universities, built for senior leadership.
detail: Analyzed 35,000+ data points across 467 metrics from 75 universities.
Ran gap analysis against 38 peer schools and 26 aspirant schools.
Built 12 Power BI visualizations presented to senior leadership.

title: Division Performance Projection — Trinity Swim & Dive
summary: Projected conference-transition placement for Trinity Swim & Dive using 2025 STAC Championship results, used directly by the coaching staff.
detail: Analyzed 192 performance records from the 2025 STAC Championship in Excel.
Built 5 Tableau visualizations projecting placement for the upcoming SAA conference transition.
Used by the coaching staff for planning.

title: Institutional Research Publication Pipeline
summary: A data pipeline pulling and cleaning ~1.2 million works from the OpenAlex API to compare Trinity's research output against 93 peer and aspirant institutions.
detail: Built a pipeline pulling publication data from the OpenAlex API.
Cleaned and loaded ~1.2 million works into a database comparing Trinity against 93 peer and aspirant institutions.
Planned: an interactive web app for deans, chairs, and faculty, plus autonomous scheduled updates.
Presenting at Trinity's summer research symposium in late July 2026.
